commands.review_summary:
The PR changes both OpenAI-compatible completions request builders to emit Z.ai `thinking.type` with gated `reasoning_effort`, adds shared provider-layer mapping, updates focused tests, and refreshes the compat type comment.

PR surface: Source +49, Tests +219. Total +268 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main and v2026.6.10 still send stale Z.ai `enable_thinking` in the legacy builder, and current main lacks a Z.ai `thinking` branch in the managed completions builder. I did not run a live Z.ai request in this read-only review.

target.title: fix(zai): send current GLM thinking params
target.body:
Closes #97772

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users enable reasoning on Z.ai GLM models but OpenClaw sends the stale `enable_thinking` payload field, so current GLM reasoning mode is not activated for GLM-5.2 and no `reasoning_content` is returned.

## Why This Change Was Made

The OpenAI-compatible completions formatter now emits the current Z.ai thinking payload shape, `thinking: { type: "enabled" | "disabled" }`, instead of the legacy `enable_thinking` field. The shared Z.ai mapping lives with the OpenAI-compatible completions provider and is used by both request builders, so the normal stream path and the managed provider-transport path stay aligned.

GLM-5.2 receives the provider-supported `reasoning_effort` values (`high` or `max`) when thinking is enabled. `minimal`, `none`, and off-style requests send disabled thinking without `reasoning_effort`, and binary Z.ai thinking models such as GLM-5.1 keep the on/off payload without an unsupported effort field.

## User Impact

Z.ai GLM-5.2 users can turn reasoning on and have OpenClaw send the request shape expected by the current Z.ai API. Existing binary thinking models keep their current behavior and do not receive extra unsupported `reasoning_effort` parameters.

## Evidence

- Claim proved: both OpenAI-compatible completions request builders use `thinking.type`, never `enable_thinking`, for Z.ai thinking mode; GLM-5.2 maps enabled low-style levels to `reasoning_effort: "high"`, strongest OpenClaw effort to `"max"`, treats `minimal`/`none` as disabled thinking, and does not add `reasoning_effort` for binary GLM-5.1.
- Commands / artifacts:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/llm/providers/openai-completions.test.ts src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts` -> 2 Vitest shards passed; 39 + 285 tests passed.
  - `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/llm/providers/openai-completions-zai-thinking.ts src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts src/llm/providers/openai-completions.ts src/llm/providers/openai-completions.test.ts packages/llm-core/src/types.ts` -> all matched files use the correct format.
  - `git diff --check` -> no whitespace errors.
  - `python .agents\skills\autoreview\scripts\autoreview --mode local` -> clean, no accepted/actionable findings reported.
- Observed result: focused payload tests intercept the legacy `streamOpenAICompletions` request body before network and directly inspect `buildOpenAICompletionsParams` for the managed transport path, verifying the current Z.ai thinking fields and effort gating in both builders.
- ClawSweeper follow-up handled: added managed OpenAI-completions transport coverage, changed `minimal`/`none` to disabled thinking with no `reasoning_effort`, and kept the shared helper under the provider layer to avoid widening the agents surface.
- Not tested / proof gaps: no live Z.ai API call was run from this environment. I checked the local environment for common Z.ai key names (`ZAI_API_KEY`, `Z_AI_API_KEY`, `OPENCLAW_ZAI_API_KEY`, `GLM_API_KEY`, `ZHIPUAI_API_KEY`) and none were present, so this PR still does not include a real GLM-5.2 response containing `reasoning_content`.

## AI Assistance

AI-assisted: yes. Codex inspected the issue, checked duplicate PR coverage, implemented the focused fix, ran validation, addressed autoreview findings, and updated the PR after ClawSweeper feedback.
